vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-460: Dashboard rebrand: rename Mission Control, unified design system, fix light/dark mode

plan.title: Dashboard rebrand: rename Mission Control, unified design system, fix light/dark mode
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-6

narratives.Problem: The Panopticon dashboard has 3 divergent visual languages, completely broken light mode (292 hardcoded color classes ignoring CSS tokens), an overcrowded 13-item horizontal nav, and a 'Mission Control' name that collides with competing AI orchestrators. The result is a tool that looks like 3 different products stitched together.
narratives.Proposal: Complete visual rebrand: establish a T3Code-inspired semantic token architecture (OKLCH, color-mix, --alpha borders), migrate all 54 component files to semantic tokens fixing light/dark mode, replace horizontal nav with grouped collapsible sidebar, rename Mission Control to Command Deck (frontend + API), update typography to DM Sans body font, add fractal noise texture and visual polish. Delivered as one cohesive feature.

items.title: Establish semantic token architecture and CSS foundation

narrative.Action: Rewrite index.css with T3Code-inspired semantic token definitions (light + dark mode), OKLCH primary colors, color-mix() surfaces, --alpha() borders. Update tailwind.config.js to expose new tokens (background, foreground, card, primary, muted, border, etc.), update font families (DM Sans body, Space Grotesk display, SF Mono code), add radius scale. Add fractal noise texture (body::after), scrollbar styling, theme transition suppression (.no-transitions class). Update index.html with DM Sans font preloads. Update useTheme.ts to apply no-transitions class during toggle. This is the foundation everything else depends on.

subItems.title: index.css contains complete light and dark semantic token definitions matching PRD Section 3
tailwind.config.js exposes all semantic tokens as Tailwind utilities (bg-background, text-foreground, bg-card, etc.)
DM Sans loaded via Google Fonts with preconnect, body default is DM Sans
Fractal noise texture visible as subtle overlay on body in both themes
Theme toggle applies .no-transitions class briefly to prevent flash

narrative.Action: Create new Sidebar.tsx component with grouped sections (Operations, Infrastructure, Observability, System) per PRD Section 2. Implement collapse/expand (256px/48px), localStorage persistence, keyboard shortcut '[', active item highlighting (blue left border + bg-accent), hover states, and group labels. Gut Header.tsx to minimal top bar (logo + breadcrumb + controls). Update App.tsx layout from flex-col to flex-row with sidebar + main content area. Add mobile sheet behavior (slide-in from left).

subItems.title: Sidebar renders 4 groups (Operations, Infrastructure, Observability, System) with correct items and icons
Sidebar collapses to 48px icon-only mode and expands to 256px, state persisted in localStorage
Active nav item shows blue left border and accent background
Header.tsx is a minimal top bar with logo, breadcrumb, and theme toggle — no nav items

items.title: Rename Mission Control to Command Deck (frontend + server API)

narrative.Action: Rename component directory MissionControl/ to CommandDeck/. Delete mission-control.module.css (1,829 lines). Update all imports across App.tsx, Header/Sidebar, and internal component references. Rename server route file mission-control.ts to command-deck.ts, update all API endpoint paths from /api/mission-control/* to /api/command-deck/*. Update server.ts route composition. Update frontend API calls to use new endpoint paths. Update all UI labels and route paths.

subItems.title: Component directory is CommandDeck/ with no remaining MissionControl references in frontend code
mission-control.module.css is deleted, Command Deck uses global semantic tokens
Server API endpoints are /api/command-deck/* with route file renamed to command-deck.ts
No 'mission-control' or 'Mission Control' strings remain in codebase (except git history and design docs)

items.title: Migrate core layout and board components to semantic tokens

narrative.Action: Apply PRD migration table (bg-gray-900 -> bg-background, text-white -> text-foreground, etc.) to core layout and board components: KanbanBoard.tsx (31 occurrences — highest density), DetailPanelLayout.tsx, DialogProvider.tsx, PlanDialog.tsx, TerminalPanel.tsx, TerminalView.tsx, XTerminal.tsx, ActivityPanel.tsx, IssueAgentCard.tsx, SpecialistAgentCard.tsx. Also apply font-medium everywhere and ensure Space Grotesk is restricted to brand text and numeric stats only.

subItems.title: KanbanBoard.tsx has zero hardcoded color classes (bg-gray-*, text-white, border-gray-*, etc.)
All core layout components use semantic tokens exclusively
Board view renders correctly in both light and dark mode with no white-on-white or invisible text

items.title: Migrate all remaining page and feature components to semantic tokens

narrative.Action: Continue migration table application across all remaining component files: AgentList, AgentDetailView, InspectorPanel (24 occurrences), inspector/* subcomponents, HealthDashboard, HealthHistoryTimeline, MetricsPage, MetricsSummary, MetricsSummaryRow, CostsPage, CostBreakdownModal, ResourcesPanel, ResourceCard, ConvoyPanel, HandoffsPage, HandoffPanel, SkillsList, BeadsDialog, BeadsTasksPanel, ContainerDetailPanel, CloisterStatusBar, RuntimeComparison, TldrServiceStatus, search/SearchModal, search/SearchResults, skeletons/*, vbrief/* (6 files), pages/SpecialistDetail, pages/SpecialistRunLog.

subItems.title: Zero hardcoded color classes remain in any frontend component file (grep confirms no bg-gray-*, text-white, border-gray-*, etc.)
All pages render correctly in both light and dark mode

items.title: Migrate Settings and chat components to semantic tokens

narrative.Action: Migrate Settings/* components (SettingsPage 14 occurrences, ProviderCard, AgentCard, ModelOverrideModal 10 occurrences, OpenRouterPage, SmartSelectionExplainer) and all chat/* components (9 files: ConversationPanel, DraftConversationPanel, ComposerFooter, ComposerPromptEditor, EffortPicker, ModelPicker, MessagesTimeline, ChatMarkdown) to semantic tokens. These are part of Command Deck and must match the new design language.

subItems.title: Settings page renders correctly in both light and dark mode
Chat/conversation components use semantic tokens and render in both themes

items.title: Migrate God View to scoped semantic token overrides

narrative.Action: Replace hardcoded hex values in GodView/theme.css (#0a0e1a, #00d4ff, etc.) with scoped overrides of the semantic tokens (--background, --foreground, --primary, --border, etc.) inside a .god-view scope. Keep the cyberpunk neon aesthetic but build it on the token foundation so it inherits structural consistency. Verify all God View components render correctly with the scoped overrides.

subItems.title: GodView/theme.css uses scoped semantic token overrides instead of hardcoded hex values
God View retains its cyberpunk visual identity (neon colors, glow effects, scanline animation)

items.title: Unify card, badge, button, and dialog patterns across all views

narrative.Action: Apply consistent component patterns from PRD Section 6 across all views: cards get rounded-2xl with opacity-based borders and inner shadows, badges use semantic bg at /8 opacity, buttons follow the primary/secondary/ghost/destructive variant pattern with correct sizes, dialogs center on viewport with backdrop blur and scale animation (fixes current right-anchor positioning bug in PlanDialog). Ensure status indicators are consistent (dot OR badge, not mixed) across all views.

subItems.title: All cards use rounded-2xl, border-border, bg-card with inner shadow pattern
PlanDialog and all dialogs center on viewport (no right-anchor positioning bug)
Badges use semantic color at /8 opacity pattern consistently

items.title: Verify all pages in both themes, run quality gates

narrative.Action: Run npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm test. Build the project (npm run build). Visually verify every page in both light and dark mode: Board, Command Deck, Agents, Resources, Convoys, Handoffs, Activity, Metrics, Costs, Health, Skills, Settings, God View. Check dialog positioning. Verify sidebar collapse/expand. Confirm no remaining hardcoded color classes via grep. Fix any issues found.

subItems.title: npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
npm run lint passes
npm test passes (root + frontend)
npm run build succeeds
grep confirms zero hardcoded color classes (bg-gray-*, text-white, border-gray-*) in frontend src
